title: Transit Committee - May 14, 2026
meeting_location: Champlain Room, 110 Laurier Avenue West, and by electronic participation

agenda_items.title: Notices and meeting information for meeting participants and the public

attachments.attachment_title: Public Notices English - Hybrid Standing Committee 2025 (AODA).pdf
Public Notices French - Hybrid Standing Committee 2025 (AODA).pdf

agenda_items.description:
Notices and meeting information are attached to the agenda and minutes, including: availability of simultaneous interpretation and accessibility accommodations; in camera meeting procedures; information items not subject to discussion; personal information disclaimer for correspondents and public speakers; notices regarding minutes; and hybrid participation details.
Accessible formats and communication supports are available, upon request.
Except where otherwise indicated, reports requiring Council consideration will be presented to Council on Wednesday, May 27, 2026 in Transit Committee Report 26.
The deadline to register by phone to speak, or submit written comments or visual presentations is 4 pm on Wednesday, May 13, 2026, and the deadline to register to speak by email is 8:30 am on Thursday, May 14, 2026.
agenda_items.minutes: These “Summary Minutes” indicate the disposition of items and actions taken at the meeting. This document does not include all of the text that will be included in the full Minutes, such as the record of written and oral submissions. Recorded votes and dissents contained in the Summary Minutes are draft until the full Minutes of the meeting are confirmed by the Committee. The draft of the full Minutes (for confirmation) will be published with the agenda for the next regular Committee meeting and, once confirmed, will replace this document.

agenda_items.title: Declarations of Interest
agenda_items.minutes: No Declarations of Interest were filed.

sub_agendas_items.title: TC-2026-05 - Number of conventional buses that have caught fire

attachments.attachment_title: TC-2026-05 - M. Carr - Number of conventional buses that have caught fire.pdf

sub_agendas_items.minutes:
Within the will of Committee, the item was lifted for discussion.
Sabrina Pasian, Chief Safety Officer, Transit Services Department responded to questions from Committee.

sub_agendas_items.title: OC Transpo Update – Rail, Bus, and Para Transpo
sub_agendas_items.minutes:
The following Transit Services Department staff provided a presentation on the item, and answered questions from the Committee. A copy of the slide presentation is filed with the Office of the City Clerk.
Noah Heulitt (Vice President of Services North America, Alstom) and Grant Bailie (Rail Transit CEO, Rideau Transit Group) answered questions from the Committee.
Taffy Nahas, Legal Counsel, Legal Services also responded to questions from Committee.
The resident Ajay Ramachandran spoke before the Committee and provided comments on the item.
Written correspondence was received from Jay Hunt, dated May 14, 2026.

sub_agendas_items.title: TC-2026-06 - Complaint from customers waiting at the bus stop
sub_agendas_items.minutes:
Submitted by Councillor M. Carr (on behalf of Councillor S. Plante):
Over the last two years (May 2024 to May 2026), how many times has OC Transpo received a complaint from a customer who was waiting at the bus stop and the bus did not stop to pick the individual up.
Please only include complaints where the individual was at a bus stop, and not at a bus station, and where the individual was at the bus stop when the bus passed by (as opposed to missing it because they were running or walking towards it).
